mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces household consumption and corporate earnings, producing negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns dominate in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad consumer demand contraction that triggers immediate negative market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of cases when both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2.0 percent quarter-over-quarter signals robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports positive equity returns during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in >60% of observations when INDPRO QoQ growth exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with stable or falling federal funds rates compresses real interest rates, boosting equity valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Real rate compression fails to produce positive 60-day returns, with Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month nonfarm payrolls growth above 150,000 signals strong labor demand that drives immediate positive market sentiment and short-term equity gains.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth exceeding 150,000 correlates with negative 5-day returns in >55% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent quarter-over-quarter indicates demand stagnation that prompts equity market sell-offs over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns dominate in >60% of quarters when GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while employment falls signals productivity-driven supply expansion that generates short-term positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Productivity divergence correlates with negative 5-day returns in >60% of monthly observations.
